4500	if (ControlFile->toast_max_chunk_size != TOAST_MAX_CHUNK_SIZE)
4501		ereport(FATAL,
4502				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
4503				 errmsg("database files are incompatible with server"),
4504		/* translator: %s is a variable name and %d is its value */
4505				 errdetail("The database cluster was initialized with %s %d,"
4506						   " but the server was compiled with %s %d.",
4507						   "TOAST_MAX_CHUNK_SIZE", ControlFile->toast_max_chunk_size,
4508						   "TOAST_MAX_CHUNK_SIZE", (int) TOAST_MAX_CHUNK_SIZE),
4509				 errhint("It looks like you need to recompile or initdb.")));
4510	if (ControlFile->loblksize != LOBLKSIZE)
4511		ereport(FATAL,
4512				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
4513				 errmsg("database files are incompatible with server"),
4514		/* translator: %s is a variable name and %d is its value */
4515				 errdetail("The database cluster was initialized with %s %d,"
4516						   " but the server was compiled with %s %d.",
4517						   "LOBLKSIZE", ControlFile->loblksize,
4518						   "LOBLKSIZE", (int) LOBLKSIZE),
4519				 errhint("It looks like you need to recompile or initdb.")));
4520
4521#ifdef USE_FLOAT8_BYVAL
4522	if (ControlFile->float8ByVal != true)
4523		ereport(FATAL,
4524				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
4525				 errmsg("database files are incompatible with server"),
4526				 errdetail("The database cluster was initialized without USE_FLOAT8_BYVAL"
4527						   " but the server was compiled with USE_FLOAT8_BYVAL."),
4528				 errhint("It looks like you need to recompile or initdb.")));
4529#else
4530	if (ControlFile->float8ByVal != false)
4531		ereport(FATAL,
4532				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
4533				 errmsg("database files are incompatible with server"),
4534				 errdetail("The database cluster was initialized with USE_FLOAT8_BYVAL"
4535						   " but the server was compiled without USE_FLOAT8_BYVAL."),
4536				 errhint("It looks like you need to recompile or initdb.")));
4537#endif
4538
4539	wal_segment_size = ControlFile->xlog_seg_size;
4540
4541	if (!IsValidWalSegSize(wal_segment_size))
4542		ereport(ERROR, (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
4543						errmsg_plural("invalid WAL segment size in control file (%d byte)",
4544									  "invalid WAL segment size in control file (%d bytes)",
4545									  wal_segment_size,
4546									  wal_segment_size),
4547						errdetail("The WAL segment size must be a power of two between 1 MB and 1 GB.")));
4548
4549	snprintf(wal_segsz_str, sizeof(wal_segsz_str), "%d", wal_segment_size);
4550	SetConfigOption("wal_segment_size", wal_segsz_str, PGC_INTERNAL,
4551					PGC_S_DYNAMIC_DEFAULT);
4552
4553	/* check and update variables dependent on wal_segment_size */
4554	if (ConvertToXSegs(min_wal_size_mb, wal_segment_size) < 2)
4555		ereport(ERROR, (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
4556		/* translator: both %s are GUC names */
4557						errmsg("\"%s\" must be at least twice \"%s\"",
4558							   "min_wal_size", "wal_segment_size")));
4559
4560	if (ConvertToXSegs(max_wal_size_mb, wal_segment_size) < 2)
4561		ereport(ERROR, (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
4562		/* translator: both %s are GUC names */
4563						errmsg("\"%s\" must be at least twice \"%s\"",
4564							   "max_wal_size", "wal_segment_size")));
4565
4566	UsableBytesInSegment =
4567		(wal_segment_size / XLOG_BLCKSZ * UsableBytesInPage) -
4568		(SizeOfXLogLongPHD - SizeOfXLogShortPHD);
4569
4570	CalculateCheckpointSegments();
4571
4572	/* Make the initdb settings visible as GUC variables, too */
4573	SetConfigOption("data_checksums", DataChecksumsEnabled() ? "yes" : "no",
4574					PGC_INTERNAL, PGC_S_DYNAMIC_DEFAULT);
4575}
4576
4577/*
4578 * Utility wrapper to update the control file.  Note that the control
4579 * file gets flushed.
4580 */
4581static void
4582UpdateControlFile(void)
4583{
4584	update_controlfile(DataDir, ControlFile, true);
4585}
4586
4587/*
4588 * Returns the unique system identifier from control file.
4589 */
4590uint64
4591GetSystemIdentifier(void)
4592{
4593	Assert(ControlFile != NULL);
4594	return ControlFile->system_identifier;
4595}
4596
4597/*
4598 * Returns the random nonce from control file.
4599 */
4600char *
4601GetMockAuthenticationNonce(void)
4602{
4603	Assert(ControlFile != NULL);
4604	return ControlFile->mock_authentication_nonce;
4605}
4606
4607/*
4608 * Are checksums enabled for data pages?
4609 */
4610bool
4611DataChecksumsEnabled(void)
4612{
4613	Assert(ControlFile != NULL);
4614	return (ControlFile->data_checksum_version > 0);
4615}
4616
4617/*
4618 * Return true if the cluster was initialized on a platform where the
4619 * default signedness of char is "signed". This function exists for code
4620 * that deals with pre-v18 data files that store data sorted by the 'char'
4621 * type on disk (e.g., GIN and GiST indexes). See the comments in
4622 * WriteControlFile() for details.
4623 */
4624bool
4625GetDefaultCharSignedness(void)
4626{
4627	return ControlFile->default_char_signedness;
4628}
4629
4630/*
4631 * Returns a fake LSN for unlogged relations.
4632 *
4633 * Each call generates an LSN that is greater than any previous value
4634 * returned. The current counter value is saved and restored across clean
4635 * shutdowns, but like unlogged relations, does not survive a crash. This can
4636 * be used in lieu of real LSN values returned by XLogInsert, if you need an
4637 * LSN-like increasing sequence of numbers without writing any WAL.
4638 */
4639XLogRecPtr
4640GetFakeLSNForUnloggedRel(void)
4641{
4642	return pg_atomic_fetch_add_u64(&XLogCtl->unloggedLSN, 1);
4643}
4644
4645/*
4646 * Auto-tune the number of XLOG buffers.
4647 *
4648 * The preferred setting for wal_buffers is about 3% of shared_buffers, with
4649 * a maximum of one XLOG segment (there is little reason to think that more
4650 * is helpful, at least so long as we force an fsync when switching log files)
4651 * and a minimum of 8 blocks (which was the default value prior to PostgreSQL
4652 * 9.1, when auto-tuning was added).
4653 *
4654 * This should not be called until NBuffers has received its final value.
4655 */
4656static int
4657XLOGChooseNumBuffers(void)
4658{
4659	int			xbuffers;
4660
4661	xbuffers = NBuffers / 32;
4662	if (xbuffers > (wal_segment_size / XLOG_BLCKSZ))
4663		xbuffers = (wal_segment_size / XLOG_BLCKSZ);
4664	if (xbuffers < 8)
4665		xbuffers = 8;
4666	return xbuffers;
4667}
4668
4669/*
4670 * GUC check_hook for wal_buffers
4671 */
4672bool
4673check_wal_buffers(int *newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
4674{
4675	/*
4676	 * -1 indicates a request for auto-tune.
4677	 */
4678	if (*newval == -1)
4679	{
4680		/*
4681		 * If we haven't yet changed the boot_val default of -1, just let it
4682		 * be.  We'll fix it when XLOGShmemSize is called.
4683		 */
4684		if (XLOGbuffers == -1)
4685			return true;
4686
4687		/* Otherwise, substitute the auto-tune value */
4688		*newval = XLOGChooseNumBuffers();
4689	}
4690
4691	/*
4692	 * We clamp manually-set values to at least 4 blocks.  Prior to PostgreSQL
4693	 * 9.1, a minimum of 4 was enforced by guc.c, but since that is no longer
4694	 * the case, we just silently treat such values as a request for the
4695	 * minimum.  (We could throw an error instead, but that doesn't seem very
4696	 * helpful.)
4697	 */
4698	if (*newval < 4)
4699		*newval = 4;
4700
4701	return true;
4702}
4703
4704/*
4705 * GUC check_hook for wal_consistency_checking
4706 */
4707bool
4708check_wal_consistency_checking(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
4709{
4710	char	   *rawstring;
4711	List	   *elemlist;
4712	ListCell   *l;
4713	bool		newwalconsistency[RM_MAX_ID + 1];
4714
4715	/* Initialize the array */
4716	MemSet(newwalconsistency, 0, (RM_MAX_ID + 1) * sizeof(bool));
4717
4718	/* Need a modifiable copy of string */
4719	rawstring = pstrdup(*newval);
4720
4721	/* Parse string into list of identifiers */
4722	if (!SplitIdentifierString(rawstring, ',', &elemlist))
4723	{
4724		/* syntax error in list */
4725		GUC_check_errdetail("List syntax is invalid.");
4726		pfree(rawstring);
4727		list_free(elemlist);
4728		return false;
4729	}
4730
4731	foreach(l, elemlist)
4732	{
4733		char	   *tok = (char *) lfirst(l);
4734		int			rmid;
4735
4736		/* Check for 'all'. */
4737		if (pg_strcasecmp(tok, "all") == 0)
4738		{
4739			for (rmid = 0; rmid <= RM_MAX_ID; rmid++)
4740				if (RmgrIdExists(rmid) && GetRmgr(rmid).rm_mask != NULL)
4741					newwalconsistency[rmid] = true;
4742		}
4743		else
4744		{
4745			/* Check if the token matches any known resource manager. */
4746			bool		found = false;
4747
4748			for (rmid = 0; rmid <= RM_MAX_ID; rmid++)
4749			{
4750				if (RmgrIdExists(rmid) && GetRmgr(rmid).rm_mask != NULL &&
4751					pg_strcasecmp(tok, GetRmgr(rmid).rm_name) == 0)
4752				{
4753					newwalconsistency[rmid] = true;
4754					found = true;
4755					break;
4756				}
4757			}
4758			if (!found)
4759			{
4760				/*
4761				 * During startup, it might be a not-yet-loaded custom
4762				 * resource manager.  Defer checking until
4763				 * InitializeWalConsistencyChecking().
4764				 */
4765				if (!process_shared_preload_libraries_done)
4766				{
4767					check_wal_consistency_checking_deferred = true;
4768				}
4769				else
4770				{
4771					GUC_check_errdetail("Unrecognized key word: \"%s\".", tok);
4772					pfree(rawstring);
4773					list_free(elemlist);
4774					return false;
4775				}
4776			}
4777		}
4778	}
4779
4780	pfree(rawstring);
4781	list_free(elemlist);
4782
4783	/* assign new value */
4784	*extra = guc_malloc(LOG, (RM_MAX_ID + 1) * sizeof(bool));
4785	if (!*extra)
4786		return false;
4787	memcpy(*extra, newwalconsistency, (RM_MAX_ID + 1) * sizeof(bool));
4788	return true;
4789}
4790
4791/*
4792 * GUC assign_hook for wal_consistency_checking
4793 */
4794void
4795assign_wal_consistency_checking(const char *newval, void *extra)
4796{
4797	/*
4798	 * If some checks were deferred, it's possible that the checks will fail
4799	 * later during InitializeWalConsistencyChecking(). But in that case, the
4800	 * postmaster will exit anyway, so it's safe to proceed with the
4801	 * assignment.
4802	 *
4803	 * Any built-in resource managers specified are assigned immediately,
4804	 * which affects WAL created before shared_preload_libraries are
4805	 * processed. Any custom resource managers specified won't be assigned
4806	 * until after shared_preload_libraries are processed, but that's OK
4807	 * because WAL for a custom resource manager can't be written before the
4808	 * module is loaded anyway.
4809	 */
4810	wal_consistency_checking = extra;
4811}
4812
4813/*
4814 * InitializeWalConsistencyChecking: run after loading custom resource managers
4815 *
4816 * If any unknown resource managers were specified in the
4817 * wal_consistency_checking GUC, processing was deferred.  Now that
4818 * shared_preload_libraries have been loaded, process wal_consistency_checking
4819 * again.
4820 */
4821void
4822InitializeWalConsistencyChecking(void)
4823{
4824	Assert(process_shared_preload_libraries_done);
4825
4826	if (check_wal_consistency_checking_deferred)
4827	{
4828		struct config_generic *guc;
4829
4830		guc = find_option("wal_consistency_checking", false, false, ERROR);
4831
4832		check_wal_consistency_checking_deferred = false;
4833
4834		set_config_option_ext("wal_consistency_checking",
4835							  wal_consistency_checking_string,
4836							  guc->scontext, guc->source, guc->srole,
4837							  GUC_ACTION_SET, true, ERROR, false);
4838
4839		/* checking should not be deferred again */
4840		Assert(!check_wal_consistency_checking_deferred);
4841	}
4842}
4843
4844/*
4845 * GUC show_hook for archive_command
4846 */
4847const char *
4848show_archive_command(void)
4849{
4850	if (XLogArchivingActive())
4851		return XLogArchiveCommand;
4852	else
4853		return "(disabled)";
4854}
4855
4856/*
4857 * GUC show_hook for in_hot_standby
4858 */
4859const char *
4860show_in_hot_standby(void)
4861{
4862	/*
4863	 * We display the actual state based on shared memory, so that this GUC
4864	 * reports up-to-date state if examined intra-query.  The underlying
4865	 * variable (in_hot_standby_guc) changes only when we transmit a new value
4866	 * to the client.
4867	 */
4868	return RecoveryInProgress() ? "on" : "off";
4869}
4870
4871/*
4872 * Read the control file, set respective GUCs.
4873 *
4874 * This is to be called during startup, including a crash recovery cycle,
4875 * unless in bootstrap mode, where no control file yet exists.  As there's no
4876 * usable shared memory yet (its sizing can depend on the contents of the
4877 * control file!), first store the contents in local memory. XLOGShmemInit()
4878 * will then copy it to shared memory later.
4879 *
4880 * reset just controls whether previous contents are to be expected (in the
4881 * reset case, there's a dangling pointer into old shared memory), or not.
4882 */
4883void
4884LocalProcessControlFile(bool reset)
4885{
4886	Assert(reset || ControlFile == NULL);
4887	ControlFile = palloc(sizeof(ControlFileData));
4888	ReadControlFile();
4889}
4890
4891/*
4892 * Get the wal_level from the control file. For a standby, this value should be
4893 * considered as its active wal_level, because it may be different from what
4894 * was originally configured on standby.
4895 */
4896WalLevel
4897GetActiveWalLevelOnStandby(void)
4898{
4899	return ControlFile->wal_level;
4900}
4901
4902/*
4903 * Initialization of shared memory for XLOG
4904 */
4905Size
4906XLOGShmemSize(void)
4907{
4908	Size		size;
4909
4910	/*
4911	 * If the value of wal_buffers is -1, use the preferred auto-tune value.
4912	 * This isn't an amazingly clean place to do this, but we must wait till
4913	 * NBuffers has received its final value, and must do it before using the
4914	 * value of XLOGbuffers to do anything important.
4915	 *
4916	 * We prefer to report this value's source as PGC_S_DYNAMIC_DEFAULT.
4917	 * However, if the DBA explicitly set wal_buffers = -1 in the config file,
4918	 * then PGC_S_DYNAMIC_DEFAULT will fail to override that and we must force
4919	 * the matter with PGC_S_OVERRIDE.
4920	 */
4921	if (XLOGbuffers == -1)
4922	{
4923		char		buf[32];
4924
4925		snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "%d", XLOGChooseNumBuffers());
4926		SetConfigOption("wal_buffers", buf, PGC_POSTMASTER,
4927						PGC_S_DYNAMIC_DEFAULT);
4928		if (XLOGbuffers == -1)	/* failed to apply it? */
4929			SetConfigOption("wal_buffers", buf, PGC_POSTMASTER,
4930							PGC_S_OVERRIDE);
4931	}
4932	Assert(XLOGbuffers > 0);
4933
4934	/* XLogCtl */
4935	size = sizeof(XLogCtlData);
4936
4937	/* WAL insertion locks, plus alignment */
4938	size = add_size(size, mul_size(sizeof(WALInsertLockPadded), NUM_XLOGINSERT_LOCKS + 1));
4939	/* xlblocks array */
4940	size = add_size(size, mul_size(sizeof(pg_atomic_uint64), XLOGbuffers));
4941	/* extra alignment padding for XLOG I/O buffers */
4942	size = add_size(size, Max(XLOG_BLCKSZ, PG_IO_ALIGN_SIZE));
4943	/* and the buffers themselves */
4944	size = add_size(size, mul_size(XLOG_BLCKSZ, XLOGbuffers));
4945
4946	/*
4947	 * Note: we don't count ControlFileData, it comes out of the "slop factor"
4948	 * added by CreateSharedMemoryAndSemaphores.  This lets us use this
4949	 * routine again below to compute the actual allocation size.
4950	 */
4951
4952	return size;
4953}
4954
4955void
4956XLOGShmemInit(void)
4957{
4958	bool		foundCFile,
4959				foundXLog;
4960	char	   *allocptr;
4961	int			i;
4962	ControlFileData *localControlFile;
4963
4964#ifdef WAL_DEBUG
4965
4966	/*
4967	 * Create a memory context for WAL debugging that's exempt from the normal
4968	 * "no pallocs in critical section" rule. Yes, that can lead to a PANIC if
4969	 * an allocation fails, but wal_debug is not for production use anyway.
4970	 */
4971	if (walDebugCxt == NULL)
4972	{
4973		walDebugCxt = AllocSetContextCreate(TopMemoryContext,
4974											"WAL Debug",
4975											ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
4976		MemoryContextAllowInCriticalSection(walDebugCxt, true);
4977	}
4978#endif
4979
4980
4981	XLogCtl = (XLogCtlData *)
4982		ShmemInitStruct("XLOG Ctl", XLOGShmemSize(), &foundXLog);
4983
4984	localControlFile = ControlFile;
4985	ControlFile = (ControlFileData *)
4986		ShmemInitStruct("Control File", sizeof(ControlFileData), &foundCFile);
4987
4988	if (foundCFile || foundXLog)
4989	{
4990		/* both should be present or neither */
4991		Assert(foundCFile && foundXLog);
4992
4993		/* Initialize local copy of WALInsertLocks */
4994		WALInsertLocks = XLogCtl->Insert.WALInsertLocks;
4995
4996		if (localControlFile)
4997			pfree(localControlFile);
4998		return;
4999	}
5000	memset(XLogCtl, 0, sizeof(XLogCtlData));
5001
5002	/*
5003	 * Already have read control file locally, unless in bootstrap mode. Move
5004	 * contents into shared memory.
5005	 */
5006	if (localControlFile)
5007	{
5008		memcpy(ControlFile, localControlFile, sizeof(ControlFileData));
5009		pfree(localControlFile);
5010	}
5011
5012	/*
5013	 * Since XLogCtlData contains XLogRecPtr fields, its sizeof should be a
5014	 * multiple of the alignment for same, so no extra alignment padding is
5015	 * needed here.
5016	 */
5017	allocptr = ((char *) XLogCtl) + sizeof(XLogCtlData);
5018	XLogCtl->xlblocks = (pg_atomic_uint64 *) allocptr;
5019	allocptr += sizeof(pg_atomic_uint64) * XLOGbuffers;
5020
5021	for (i = 0; i < XLOGbuffers; i++)
5022	{
5023		pg_atomic_init_u64(&XLogCtl->xlblocks[i], InvalidXLogRecPtr);
5024	}
5025
5026	/* WAL insertion locks. Ensure they're aligned to the full padded size */
5027	allocptr += sizeof(WALInsertLockPadded) -
5028		((uintptr_t) allocptr) % sizeof(WALInsertLockPadded);
5029	WALInsertLocks = XLogCtl->Insert.WALInsertLocks =
5030		(WALInsertLockPadded *) allocptr;
5031	allocptr += sizeof(WALInsertLockPadded) * NUM_XLOGINSERT_LOCKS;
5032
5033	for (i = 0; i < NUM_XLOGINSERT_LOCKS; i++)
5034	{
5035		LWLockInitialize(&WALInsertLocks[i].l.lock, LWTRANCHE_WAL_INSERT);
5036		pg_atomic_init_u64(&WALInsertLocks[i].l.insertingAt, InvalidXLogRecPtr);
5037		WALInsertLocks[i].l.lastImportantAt = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
5038	}
5039
5040	/*
5041	 * Align the start of the page buffers to a full xlog block size boundary.
5042	 * This simplifies some calculations in XLOG insertion. It is also
5043	 * required for O_DIRECT.
5044	 */
5045	allocptr = (char *) TYPEALIGN(XLOG_BLCKSZ, allocptr);
5046	XLogCtl->pages = allocptr;
5047	memset(XLogCtl->pages, 0, (Size) XLOG_BLCKSZ * XLOGbuffers);
5048
5049	/*
5050	 * Do basic initialization of XLogCtl shared data. (StartupXLOG will fill
5051	 * in additional info.)
5052	 */
5053	XLogCtl->XLogCacheBlck = XLOGbuffers - 1;
5054	XLogCtl->SharedRecoveryState = RECOVERY_STATE_CRASH;
5055	XLogCtl->InstallXLogFileSegmentActive = false;
5056	XLogCtl->WalWriterSleeping = false;
5057
5058	SpinLockInit(&XLogCtl->Insert.insertpos_lck);
5059	SpinLockInit(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
5060	pg_atomic_init_u64(&XLogCtl->logInsertResult, InvalidXLogRecPtr);
5061	pg_atomic_init_u64(&XLogCtl->logWriteResult, InvalidXLogRecPtr);
5062	pg_atomic_init_u64(&XLogCtl->logFlushResult, InvalidXLogRecPtr);
5063	pg_atomic_init_u64(&XLogCtl->unloggedLSN, InvalidXLogRecPtr);
5064}
5065
5066/*
5067 * This func must be called ONCE on system install.  It creates pg_control
5068 * and the initial XLOG segment.
5069 */
5070void
5071BootStrapXLOG(uint32 data_checksum_version)
5072{
5073	CheckPoint	checkPoint;
5074	char	   *buffer;
5075	XLogPageHeader page;
5076	XLogLongPageHeader longpage;
5077	XLogRecord *record;
5078	char	   *recptr;
5079	uint64		sysidentifier;
5080	struct timeval tv;
5081	pg_crc32c	crc;
5082
5083	/* allow ordinary WAL segment creation, like StartupXLOG() would */
5084	SetInstallXLogFileSegmentActive();
5085
5086	/*
5087	 * Select a hopefully-unique system identifier code for this installation.
5088	 * We use the result of gettimeofday(), including the fractional seconds
5089	 * field, as being about as unique as we can easily get.  (Think not to
5090	 * use random(), since it hasn't been seeded and there's no portable way
5091	 * to seed it other than the system clock value...)  The upper half of the
5092	 * uint64 value is just the tv_sec part, while the lower half contains the
5093	 * tv_usec part (which must fit in 20 bits), plus 12 bits from our current
5094	 * PID for a little extra uniqueness.  A person knowing this encoding can
5095	 * determine the initialization time of the installation, which could
5096	 * perhaps be useful sometimes.
5097	 */
5098	gettimeofday(&tv, NULL);
5099	sysidentifier = ((uint64) tv.tv_sec) << 32;
5100	sysidentifier |= ((uint64) tv.tv_usec) << 12;
5101	sysidentifier |= getpid() & 0xFFF;
5102
5103	/* page buffer must be aligned suitably for O_DIRECT */
5104	buffer = (char *) palloc(XLOG_BLCKSZ + XLOG_BLCKSZ);
5105	page = (XLogPageHeader) TYPEALIGN(XLOG_BLCKSZ, buffer);
5106	memset(page, 0, XLOG_BLCKSZ);
5107
5108	/*
5109	 * Set up information for the initial checkpoint record
5110	 *
5111	 * The initial checkpoint record is written to the beginning of the WAL
5112	 * segment with logid=0 logseg=1. The very first WAL segment, 0/0, is not
5113	 * used, so that we can use 0/0 to mean "before any valid WAL segment".
5114	 */
5115	checkPoint.redo = wal_segment_size + SizeOfXLogLongPHD;
5116	checkPoint.ThisTimeLineID = BootstrapTimeLineID;
5117	checkPoint.PrevTimeLineID = BootstrapTimeLineID;
5118	checkPoint.fullPageWrites = fullPageWrites;
5119	checkPoint.wal_level = wal_level;
5120	checkPoint.nextXid =
5121		FullTransactionIdFromEpochAndXid(0, FirstNormalTransactionId);
5122	checkPoint.nextOid = FirstGenbkiObjectId;
5123	checkPoint.nextMulti = FirstMultiXactId;
5124	checkPoint.nextMultiOffset = 0;
5125	checkPoint.oldestXid = FirstNormalTransactionId;
5126	checkPoint.oldestXidDB = Template1DbOid;
5127	checkPoint.oldestMulti = FirstMultiXactId;
5128	checkPoint.oldestMultiDB = Template1DbOid;
5129	checkPoint.oldestCommitTsXid = InvalidTransactionId;
5130	checkPoint.newestCommitTsXid = InvalidTransactionId;
5131	checkPoint.time = (pg_time_t) time(NULL);
5132	checkPoint.oldestActiveXid = InvalidTransactionId;
5133
5134	TransamVariables->nextXid = checkPoint.nextXid;
5135	TransamVariables->nextOid = checkPoint.nextOid;
5136	TransamVariables->oidCount = 0;
5137	MultiXactSetNextMXact(checkPoint.nextMulti, checkPoint.nextMultiOffset);
5138	AdvanceOldestClogXid(checkPoint.oldestXid);
5139	SetTransactionIdLimit(checkPoint.oldestXid, checkPoint.oldestXidDB);
5140	SetMultiXactIdLimit(checkPoint.oldestMulti, checkPoint.oldestMultiDB, true);
5141	SetCommitTsLimit(InvalidTransactionId, InvalidTransactionId);
5142
5143	/* Set up the XLOG page header */
5144	page->xlp_magic = XLOG_PAGE_MAGIC;
5145	page->xlp_info = XLP_LONG_HEADER;
5146	page->xlp_tli = BootstrapTimeLineID;
5147	page->xlp_pageaddr = wal_segment_size;
5148	longpage = (XLogLongPageHeader) page;
5149	longpage->xlp_sysid = sysidentifier;
5150	longpage->xlp_seg_size = wal_segment_size;
5151	longpage->xlp_xlog_blcksz = XLOG_BLCKSZ;
5152
5153	/* Insert the initial checkpoint record */
5154	recptr = ((char *) page + SizeOfXLogLongPHD);
5155	record = (XLogRecord *) recptr;
5156	record->xl_prev = 0;
5157	record->xl_xid = InvalidTransactionId;
5158	record->xl_tot_len = SizeOfXLogRecord + SizeOfXLogRecordDataHeaderShort + sizeof(checkPoint);
5159	record->xl_info = XLOG_CHECKPOINT_SHUTDOWN;
5160	record->xl_rmid = RM_XLOG_ID;
5161	recptr += SizeOfXLogRecord;
5162	/* fill the XLogRecordDataHeaderShort struct */
5163	*(recptr++) = (char) XLR_BLOCK_ID_DATA_SHORT;
5164	*(recptr++) = sizeof(checkPoint);
5165	memcpy(recptr, &checkPoint, sizeof(checkPoint));
5166	recptr += sizeof(checkPoint);
5167	Assert(recptr - (char *) record == record->xl_tot_len);
5168
5169	INIT_CRC32C(crc);
5170	COMP_CRC32C(crc, ((char *) record) + SizeOfXLogRecord, record->xl_tot_len - SizeOfXLogRecord);
5171	COMP_CRC32C(crc, (char *) record, offsetof(XLogRecord, xl_crc));
5172	FIN_CRC32C(crc);
5173	record->xl_crc = crc;
5174
5175	/* Create first XLOG segment file */
5176	openLogTLI = BootstrapTimeLineID;
5177	openLogFile = XLogFileInit(1, BootstrapTimeLineID);
5178
5179	/*
5180	 * We needn't bother with Reserve/ReleaseExternalFD here, since we'll
5181	 * close the file again in a moment.
5182	 */
5183
5184	/* Write the first page with the initial record */
5185	errno = 0;
5186	pgstat_report_wait_start(WAIT_EVENT_WAL_BOOTSTRAP_WRITE);
5187	if (write(openLogFile, page, XLOG_BLCKSZ) != XLOG_BLCKSZ)
5188	{
5189		/* if write didn't set errno, assume problem is no disk space */
5190		if (errno == 0)
5191			errno = ENOSPC;
5192		ereport(PANIC,
5193				(errcode_for_file_access(),
5194				 errmsg("could not write bootstrap write-ahead log file: %m")));
5195	}
5196	pgstat_report_wait_end();
5197
5198	pgstat_report_wait_start(WAIT_EVENT_WAL_BOOTSTRAP_SYNC);
5199	if (pg_fsync(openLogFile) != 0)
5200		ereport(PANIC,
5201				(errcode_for_file_access(),
5202				 errmsg("could not fsync bootstrap write-ahead log file: %m")));
5203	pgstat_report_wait_end();
5204
5205	if (close(openLogFile) != 0)
5206		ereport(PANIC,
5207				(errcode_for_file_access(),
5208				 errmsg("could not close bootstrap write-ahead log file: %m")));
5209
5210	openLogFile = -1;
5211
5212	/* Now create pg_control */
5213	InitControlFile(sysidentifier, data_checksum_version);
5214	ControlFile->time = checkPoint.time;
5215	ControlFile->checkPoint = checkPoint.redo;
5216	ControlFile->checkPointCopy = checkPoint;
5217
5218	/* some additional ControlFile fields are set in WriteControlFile() */
5219	WriteControlFile();
5220
5221	/* Bootstrap the commit log, too */
5222	BootStrapCLOG();
5223	BootStrapCommitTs();
5224	BootStrapSUBTRANS();
5225	BootStrapMultiXact();
5226
5227	pfree(buffer);
5228
5229	/*
5230	 * Force control file to be read - in contrast to normal processing we'd
5231	 * otherwise never run the checks and GUC related initializations therein.
5232	 */
5233	ReadControlFile();
5234}
5235
5236static char *
5237str_time(pg_time_t tnow)
5238{
5239	char	   *buf = palloc(128);
5240
5241	pg_strftime(buf, 128,
5242				"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z",
5243				pg_localtime(&tnow, log_timezone));
5244
5245	return buf;
5246}
5247
5248/*
5249 * Initialize the first WAL segment on new timeline.
5250 */
5251static void
5252XLogInitNewTimeline(TimeLineID endTLI, XLogRecPtr endOfLog, TimeLineID newTLI)
5253{
5254	char		xlogfname[MAXFNAMELEN];
5255	XLogSegNo	endLogSegNo;
5256	XLogSegNo	startLogSegNo;
5257
5258	/* we always switch to a new timeline after archive recovery */
5259	Assert(endTLI != newTLI);
5260
5261	/*
5262	 * Update min recovery point one last time.
5263	 */
5264	UpdateMinRecoveryPoint(InvalidXLogRecPtr, true);
5265
5266	/*
5267	 * Calculate the last segment on the old timeline, and the first segment
5268	 * on the new timeline. If the switch happens in the middle of a segment,
5269	 * they are the same, but if the switch happens exactly at a segment
5270	 * boundary, startLogSegNo will be endLogSegNo + 1.
5271	 */
5272	XLByteToPrevSeg(endOfLog, endLogSegNo, wal_segment_size);
5273	XLByteToSeg(endOfLog, startLogSegNo, wal_segment_size);
5274
5275	/*
5276	 * Initialize the starting WAL segment for the new timeline. If the switch
5277	 * happens in the middle of a segment, copy data from the last WAL segment
5278	 * of the old timeline up to the switch point, to the starting WAL segment
5279	 * on the new timeline.
5280	 */
5281	if (endLogSegNo == startLogSegNo)
5282	{
5283		/*
5284		 * Make a copy of the file on the new timeline.
5285		 *
5286		 * Writing WAL isn't allowed yet, so there are no locking
5287		 * considerations. But we should be just as tense as XLogFileInit to
5288		 * avoid emplacing a bogus file.
5289		 */
5290		XLogFileCopy(newTLI, endLogSegNo, endTLI, endLogSegNo,
5291					 XLogSegmentOffset(endOfLog, wal_segment_size));
5292	}
5293	else
5294	{
5295		/*
5296		 * The switch happened at a segment boundary, so just create the next
5297		 * segment on the new timeline.
5298		 */
5299		int			fd;
5300
5301		fd = XLogFileInit(startLogSegNo, newTLI);
5302
5303		if (close(fd) != 0)
5304		{
5305			int			save_errno = errno;
5306
5307			XLogFileName(xlogfname, newTLI, startLogSegNo, wal_segment_size);
5308			errno = save_errno;
5309			ereport(ERROR,
5310					(errcode_for_file_access(),
5311					 errmsg("could not close file \"%s\": %m", xlogfname)));
5312		}
5313	}
5314
5315	/*
5316	 * Let's just make real sure there are not .ready or .done flags posted
5317	 * for the new segment.
5318	 */
5319	XLogFileName(xlogfname, newTLI, startLogSegNo, wal_segment_size);
5320	XLogArchiveCleanup(xlogfname);
5321}
5322
5323/*
5324 * Perform cleanup actions at the conclusion of archive recovery.
5325 */
5326static void
5327CleanupAfterArchiveRecovery(TimeLineID EndOfLogTLI, XLogRecPtr EndOfLog,
5328							TimeLineID newTLI)
5329{
5330	/*
5331	 * Execute the recovery_end_command, if any.
5332	 */
5333	if (recoveryEndCommand && strcmp(recoveryEndCommand, "") != 0)
5334		ExecuteRecoveryCommand(recoveryEndCommand,
5335							   "recovery_end_command",
5336							   true,
5337							   WAIT_EVENT_RECOVERY_END_COMMAND);
5338
5339	/*
5340	 * We switched to a new timeline. Clean up segments on the old timeline.
5341	 *
5342	 * If there are any higher-numbered segments on the old timeline, remove
5343	 * them. They might contain valid WAL, but they might also be
5344	 * pre-allocated files containing garbage. In any case, they are not part
5345	 * of the new timeline's history so we don't need them.
5346	 */
5347	RemoveNonParentXlogFiles(EndOfLog, newTLI);
5348
5349	/*
5350	 * If the switch happened in the middle of a segment, what to do with the
5351	 * last, partial segment on the old timeline? If we don't archive it, and
5352	 * the server that created the WAL never archives it either (e.g. because
5353	 * it was hit by a meteor), it will never make it to the archive. That's
5354	 * OK from our point of view, because the new segment that we created with
5355	 * the new TLI contains all the WAL from the old timeline up to the switch
5356	 * point. But if you later try to do PITR to the "missing" WAL on the old
5357	 * timeline, recovery won't find it in the archive. It's physically
5358	 * present in the new file with new TLI, but recovery won't look there
5359	 * when it's recovering to the older timeline. On the other hand, if we
5360	 * archive the partial segment, and the original server on that timeline
5361	 * is still running and archives the completed version of the same segment
5362	 * later, it will fail. (We used to do that in 9.4 and below, and it
5363	 * caused such problems).
5364	 *
5365	 * As a compromise, we rename the last segment with the .partial suffix,
5366	 * and archive it. Archive recovery will never try to read .partial
5367	 * segments, so they will normally go unused. But in the odd PITR case,
5368	 * the administrator can copy them manually to the pg_wal directory
5369	 * (removing the suffix). They can be useful in debugging, too.
5370	 *
5371	 * If a .done or .ready file already exists for the old timeline, however,
5372	 * we had already determined that the segment is complete, so we can let
5373	 * it be archived normally. (In particular, if it was restored from the
5374	 * archive to begin with, it's expected to have a .done file).
5375	 */
5376	if (XLogSegmentOffset(EndOfLog, wal_segment_size) != 0 &&
5377		XLogArchivingActive())
5378	{
5379		char		origfname[MAXFNAMELEN];
5380		XLogSegNo	endLogSegNo;
5381
5382		XLByteToPrevSeg(EndOfLog, endLogSegNo, wal_segment_size);
5383		XLogFileName(origfname, EndOfLogTLI, endLogSegNo, wal_segment_size);
5384
5385		if (!XLogArchiveIsReadyOrDone(origfname))
5386		{
5387			char		origpath[MAXPGPATH];
5388			char		partialfname[MAXFNAMELEN];
5389			char		partialpath[MAXPGPATH];
5390
5391			/*
5392			 * If we're summarizing WAL, we can't rename the partial file
5393			 * until the summarizer finishes with it, else it will fail.
5394			 */
5395			if (summarize_wal)
5396				WaitForWalSummarization(EndOfLog);
5397
5398			XLogFilePath(origpath, EndOfLogTLI, endLogSegNo, wal_segment_size);
5399			snprintf(partialfname, MAXFNAMELEN, "%s.partial", origfname);
5400			snprintf(partialpath, MAXPGPATH, "%s.partial", origpath);
5401
5402			/*
5403			 * Make sure there's no .done or .ready file for the .partial
5404			 * file.
5405			 */
5406			XLogArchiveCleanup(partialfname);
5407
5408			durable_rename(origpath, partialpath, ERROR);
5409			XLogArchiveNotify(partialfname);
5410		}
5411	}
5412}
5413
5414/*
5415 * Check to see if required parameters are set high enough on this server
5416 * for various aspects of recovery operation.
5417 *
5418 * Note that all the parameters which this function tests need to be
5419 * listed in Administrator's Overview section in high-availability.sgml.
5420 * If you change them, don't forget to update the list.
5421 */
5422static void
5423CheckRequiredParameterValues(void)
5424{
5425	/*
5426	 * For archive recovery, the WAL must be generated with at least 'replica'
5427	 * wal_level.
5428	 */
5429	if (ArchiveRecoveryRequested && ControlFile->wal_level == WAL_LEVEL_MINIMAL)
5430	{
5431		ereport(FATAL,
5432				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
5433				 errmsg("WAL was generated with \"wal_level=minimal\", cannot continue recovering"),
5434				 errdetail("This happens if you temporarily set \"wal_level=minimal\" on the server."),
5435				 errhint("Use a backup taken after setting \"wal_level\" to higher than \"minimal\".")));
5436	}
5437
5438	/*
5439	 * For Hot Standby, the WAL must be generated with 'replica' mode, and we
5440	 * must have at least as many backend slots as the primary.
5441	 */
5442	if (ArchiveRecoveryRequested && EnableHotStandby)
5443	{
5444		/* We ignore autovacuum_worker_slots when we make this test. */
5445		RecoveryRequiresIntParameter("max_connections",
5446									 MaxConnections,
5447									 ControlFile->MaxConnections);
5448		RecoveryRequiresIntParameter("max_worker_processes",
5449									 max_worker_processes,
5450									 ControlFile->max_worker_processes);
5451		RecoveryRequiresIntParameter("max_wal_senders",
5452									 max_wal_senders,
5453									 ControlFile->max_wal_senders);
5454		RecoveryRequiresIntParameter("max_prepared_transactions",
5455									 max_prepared_xacts,
5456									 ControlFile->max_prepared_xacts);
5457		RecoveryRequiresIntParameter("max_locks_per_transaction",
5458									 max_locks_per_xact,
5459									 ControlFile->max_locks_per_xact);
5460	}
5461}
5462
5463/*
5464 * This must be called ONCE during postmaster or standalone-backend startup
5465 */
5466void
5467StartupXLOG(void)
5468{
5469	XLogCtlInsert *Insert;
5470	CheckPoint	checkPoint;
5471	bool		wasShutdown;
5472	bool		didCrash;
5473	bool		haveTblspcMap;
5474	bool		haveBackupLabel;
5475	XLogRecPtr	EndOfLog;
5476	TimeLineID	EndOfLogTLI;
5477	TimeLineID	newTLI;
5478	bool		performedWalRecovery;
5479	EndOfWalRecoveryInfo *endOfRecoveryInfo;
5480	XLogRecPtr	abortedRecPtr;
5481	XLogRecPtr	missingContrecPtr;
5482	TransactionId oldestActiveXID;
5483	bool		promoted = false;
5484
5485	/*
5486	 * We should have an aux process resource owner to use, and we should not
5487	 * be in a transaction that's installed some other resowner.
5488	 */
5489	Assert(AuxProcessResourceOwner != NULL);
5490	Assert(CurrentResourceOwner == NULL ||
5491		   CurrentResourceOwner == AuxProcessResourceOwner);
5492	CurrentResourceOwner = AuxProcessResourceOwner;
5493
5494	/*
5495	 * Check that contents look valid.
5496	 */
5497	if (!XRecOffIsValid(ControlFile->checkPoint))
5498		ereport(FATAL,
5499				(errcode(ERRCODE_DATA_CORRUPTED),
5500				 errmsg("control file contains invalid checkpoint location")));
5501
5502	switch (ControlFile->state)
5503	{
5504		case DB_SHUTDOWNED:
5505
5506			/*
5507			 * This is the expected case, so don't be chatty in standalone
5508			 * mode
5509			 */
5510			ereport(IsPostmasterEnvironment ? LOG : NOTICE,
5511					(errmsg("database system was shut down at %s",
5512							str_time(ControlFile->time))));
5513			break;
5514
5515		case DB_SHUTDOWNED_IN_RECOVERY:
5516			ereport(LOG,
5517					(errmsg("database system was shut down in recovery at %s",
5518							str_time(ControlFile->time))));
5519			break;
5520
5521		case DB_SHUTDOWNING:
5522			ereport(LOG,
5523					(errmsg("database system shutdown was interrupted; last known up at %s",
5524							str_time(ControlFile->time))));
5525			break;
5526
5527		case DB_IN_CRASH_RECOVERY:
5528			ereport(LOG,
5529					(errmsg("database system was interrupted while in recovery at %s",
5530							str_time(ControlFile->time)),
5531					 errhint("This probably means that some data is corrupted and"
5532							 " you will have to use the last backup for recovery.")));
5533			break;
5534
5535		case DB_IN_ARCHIVE_RECOVERY:
5536			ereport(LOG,
5537					(errmsg("database system was interrupted while in recovery at log time %s",
5538							str_time(ControlFile->checkPointCopy.time)),
5539					 errhint("If this has occurred more than once some data might be corrupted"
5540							 " and you might need to choose an earlier recovery target.")));
5541			break;
5542
5543		case DB_IN_PRODUCTION:
5544			ereport(LOG,
5545					(errmsg("database system was interrupted; last known up at %s",
5546							str_time(ControlFile->time))));
5547			break;
5548
5549		default:
5550			ereport(FATAL,
5551					(errcode(ERRCODE_DATA_CORRUPTED),
5552					 errmsg("control file contains invalid database cluster state")));
5553	}
5554
5555	/* This is just to allow attaching to startup process with a debugger */
5556#ifdef XLOG_REPLAY_DELAY
5557	if (ControlFile->state != DB_SHUTDOWNED)
5558		pg_usleep(60000000L);
5559#endif
5560
5561	/*
5562	 * Verify that pg_wal, pg_wal/archive_status, and pg_wal/summaries exist.
5563	 * In cases where someone has performed a copy for PITR, these directories
5564	 * may have been excluded and need to be re-created.
5565	 */
5566	ValidateXLOGDirectoryStructure();
5567
5568	/* Set up timeout handler needed to report startup progress. */
5569	if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
5570		RegisterTimeout(STARTUP_PROGRESS_TIMEOUT,
5571						startup_progress_timeout_handler);
5572
5573	/*----------
5574	 * If we previously crashed, perform a couple of actions:
5575	 *
5576	 * - The pg_wal directory may still include some temporary WAL segments
5577	 *   used when creating a new segment, so perform some clean up to not
5578	 *   bloat this path.  This is done first as there is no point to sync
5579	 *   this temporary data.
5580	 *
5581	 * - There might be data which we had written, intending to fsync it, but
5582	 *   which we had not actually fsync'd yet.  Therefore, a power failure in
5583	 *   the near future might cause earlier unflushed writes to be lost, even
5584	 *   though more recent data written to disk from here on would be
5585	 *   persisted.  To avoid that, fsync the entire data directory.
5586	 */
5587	if (ControlFile->state != DB_SHUTDOWNED &&
5588		ControlFile->state != DB_SHUTDOWNED_IN_RECOVERY)
5589	{
5590		RemoveTempXlogFiles();
5591		SyncDataDirectory();
5592		didCrash = true;
5593	}
5594	else
5595		didCrash = false;
5596
5597	/*
5598	 * Prepare for WAL recovery if needed.
5599	 *
5600	 * InitWalRecovery analyzes the control file and the backup label file, if
5601	 * any.  It updates the in-memory ControlFile buffer according to the
5602	 * starting checkpoint, and sets InRecovery and ArchiveRecoveryRequested.
5603	 * It also applies the tablespace map file, if any.
5604	 */
5605	InitWalRecovery(ControlFile, &wasShutdown,
5606					&haveBackupLabel, &haveTblspcMap);
5607	checkPoint = ControlFile->checkPointCopy;
5608
5609	/* initialize shared memory variables from the checkpoint record */
5610	TransamVariables->nextXid = checkPoint.nextXid;
5611	TransamVariables->nextOid = checkPoint.nextOid;
5612	TransamVariables->oidCount = 0;
5613	MultiXactSetNextMXact(checkPoint.nextMulti, checkPoint.nextMultiOffset);
5614	AdvanceOldestClogXid(checkPoint.oldestXid);
5615	SetTransactionIdLimit(checkPoint.oldestXid, checkPoint.oldestXidDB);
5616	SetMultiXactIdLimit(checkPoint.oldestMulti, checkPoint.oldestMultiDB, true);
5617	SetCommitTsLimit(checkPoint.oldestCommitTsXid,
5618					 checkPoint.newestCommitTsXid);
5619	XLogCtl->ckptFullXid = checkPoint.nextXid;
5620
5621	/*
5622	 * Clear out any old relcache cache files.  This is *necessary* if we do
5623	 * any WAL replay, since that would probably result in the cache files
5624	 * being out of sync with database reality.  In theory we could leave them
5625	 * in place if the database had been cleanly shut down, but it seems
5626	 * safest to just remove them always and let them be rebuilt during the
5627	 * first backend startup.  These files needs to be removed from all
5628	 * directories including pg_tblspc, however the symlinks are created only
5629	 * after reading tablespace_map file in case of archive recovery from
5630	 * backup, so needs to clear old relcache files here after creating
5631	 * symlinks.
5632	 */
5633	RelationCacheInitFileRemove();
5634
5635	/*
5636	 * Initialize replication slots, before there's a chance to remove
5637	 * required resources.
5638	 */
5639	StartupReplicationSlots();
5640
5641	/*
5642	 * Startup logical state, needs to be setup now so we have proper data
5643	 * during crash recovery.
5644	 */
5645	StartupReorderBuffer();
5646
5647	/*
5648	 * Startup CLOG. This must be done after TransamVariables->nextXid has
5649	 * been initialized and before we accept connections or begin WAL replay.
5650	 */
5651	StartupCLOG();
5652
5653	/*
5654	 * Startup MultiXact. We need to do this early to be able to replay
5655	 * truncations.
5656	 */
5657	StartupMultiXact();
5658
5659	/*
5660	 * Ditto for commit timestamps.  Activate the facility if the setting is
5661	 * enabled in the control file, as there should be no tracking of commit
5662	 * timestamps done when the setting was disabled.  This facility can be
5663	 * started or stopped when replaying a XLOG_PARAMETER_CHANGE record.
5664	 */
5665	if (ControlFile->track_commit_timestamp)
5666		StartupCommitTs();
5667
5668	/*
5669	 * Recover knowledge about replay progress of known replication partners.
5670	 */
5671	StartupReplicationOrigin();
5672
5673	/*
5674	 * Initialize unlogged LSN. On a clean shutdown, it's restored from the
5675	 * control file. On recovery, all unlogged relations are blown away, so
5676	 * the unlogged LSN counter can be reset too.
5677	 */
5678	if (ControlFile->state == DB_SHUTDOWNED)
5679		pg_atomic_write_membarrier_u64(&XLogCtl->unloggedLSN,
5680									   ControlFile->unloggedLSN);
5681	else
5682		pg_atomic_write_membarrier_u64(&XLogCtl->unloggedLSN,
5683									   FirstNormalUnloggedLSN);
5684
5685	/*
5686	 * Copy any missing timeline history files between 'now' and the recovery
5687	 * target timeline from archive to pg_wal. While we don't need those files
5688	 * ourselves - the history file of the recovery target timeline covers all
5689	 * the previous timelines in the history too - a cascading standby server
5690	 * might be interested in them. Or, if you archive the WAL from this
5691	 * server to a different archive than the primary, it'd be good for all
5692	 * the history files to get archived there after failover, so that you can
5693	 * use one of the old timelines as a PITR target. Timeline history files
5694	 * are small, so it's better to copy them unnecessarily than not copy them
5695	 * and regret later.
5696	 */
5697	restoreTimeLineHistoryFiles(checkPoint.ThisTimeLineID, recoveryTargetTLI);
5698
5699	/*
5700	 * Before running in recovery, scan pg_twophase and fill in its status to
5701	 * be able to work on entries generated by redo.  Doing a scan before
5702	 * taking any recovery action has the merit to discard any 2PC files that
5703	 * are newer than the first record to replay, saving from any conflicts at
5704	 * replay.  This avoids as well any subsequent scans when doing recovery
5705	 * of the on-disk two-phase data.
5706	 */
5707	restoreTwoPhaseData();
5708
5709	/*
5710	 * When starting with crash recovery, reset pgstat data - it might not be
5711	 * valid. Otherwise restore pgstat data. It's safe to do this here,
5712	 * because postmaster will not yet have started any other processes.
5713	 *
5714	 * NB: Restoring replication slot stats relies on slot state to have
5715	 * already been restored from disk.
5716	 *
5717	 * TODO: With a bit of extra work we could just start with a pgstat file
5718	 * associated with the checkpoint redo location we're starting from.
5719	 */
5720	if (didCrash)
5721		pgstat_discard_stats();
5722	else
5723		pgstat_restore_stats();
5724
5725	lastFullPageWrites = checkPoint.fullPageWrites;
5726
5727	RedoRecPtr = XLogCtl->RedoRecPtr = XLogCtl->Insert.RedoRecPtr = checkPoint.redo;
5728	doPageWrites = lastFullPageWrites;
5729
5730	/* REDO */
5731	if (InRecovery)
5732	{
5733		/* Initialize state for RecoveryInProgress() */
5734		SpinLockAcquire(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
5735		if (InArchiveRecovery)
5736			XLogCtl->SharedRecoveryState = RECOVERY_STATE_ARCHIVE;
5737		else
5738			XLogCtl->SharedRecoveryState = RECOVERY_STATE_CRASH;
5739		SpinLockRelease(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
5740
5741		/*
5742		 * Update pg_control to show that we are recovering and to show the
5743		 * selected checkpoint as the place we are starting from. We also mark
5744		 * pg_control with any minimum recovery stop point obtained from a
5745		 * backup history file.
5746		 *
5747		 * No need to hold ControlFileLock yet, we aren't up far enough.
5748		 */
5749		UpdateControlFile();
5750
5751		/*
5752		 * If there was a backup label file, it's done its job and the info
5753		 * has now been propagated into pg_control.  We must get rid of the
5754		 * label file so that if we crash during recovery, we'll pick up at
5755		 * the latest recovery restartpoint instead of going all the way back
5756		 * to the backup start point.  It seems prudent though to just rename
5757		 * the file out of the way rather than delete it completely.
5758		 */
5759		if (haveBackupLabel)
5760		{
5761			unlink(BACKUP_LABEL_OLD);
5762			durable_rename(BACKUP_LABEL_FILE, BACKUP_LABEL_OLD, FATAL);
5763		}
5764
5765		/*
5766		 * If there was a tablespace_map file, it's done its job and the
5767		 * symlinks have been created.  We must get rid of the map file so
5768		 * that if we crash during recovery, we don't create symlinks again.
5769		 * It seems prudent though to just rename the file out of the way
5770		 * rather than delete it completely.
5771		 */
5772		if (haveTblspcMap)
5773		{
5774			unlink(TABLESPACE_MAP_OLD);
5775			durable_rename(TABLESPACE_MAP, TABLESPACE_MAP_OLD, FATAL);
5776		}
5777
5778		/*
5779		 * Initialize our local copy of minRecoveryPoint.  When doing crash
5780		 * recovery we want to replay up to the end of WAL.  Particularly, in
5781		 * the case of a promoted standby minRecoveryPoint value in the
5782		 * control file is only updated after the first checkpoint.  However,
5783		 * if the instance crashes before the first post-recovery checkpoint
5784		 * is completed then recovery will use a stale location causing the
5785		 * startup process to think that there are still invalid page
5786		 * references when checking for data consistency.
5787		 */
5788		if (InArchiveRecovery)
5789		{
5790			LocalMinRecoveryPoint = ControlFile->minRecoveryPoint;
5791			LocalMinRecoveryPointTLI = ControlFile->minRecoveryPointTLI;
5792		}
5793		else
5794		{
5795			LocalMinRecoveryPoint = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
5796			LocalMinRecoveryPointTLI = 0;
5797		}
5798
5799		/* Check that the GUCs used to generate the WAL allow recovery */
5800		CheckRequiredParameterValues();
5801
5802		/*
5803		 * We're in recovery, so unlogged relations may be trashed and must be
5804		 * reset.  This should be done BEFORE allowing Hot Standby
5805		 * connections, so that read-only backends don't try to read whatever
5806		 * garbage is left over from before.
5807		 */
5808		ResetUnloggedRelations(UNLOGGED_RELATION_CLEANUP);
5809
5810		/*
5811		 * Likewise, delete any saved transaction snapshot files that got left
5812		 * behind by crashed backends.
5813		 */
5814		DeleteAllExportedSnapshotFiles();
5815
5816		/*
5817		 * Initialize for Hot Standby, if enabled. We won't let backends in
5818		 * yet, not until we've reached the min recovery point specified in
5819		 * control file and we've established a recovery snapshot from a
5820		 * running-xacts WAL record.
5821		 */
5822		if (ArchiveRecoveryRequested && EnableHotStandby)
5823		{
5824			TransactionId *xids;
5825			int			nxids;
5826
5827			ereport(DEBUG1,
5828					(errmsg_internal("initializing for hot standby")));
5829
5830			InitRecoveryTransactionEnvironment();
5831
5832			if (wasShutdown)
5833				oldestActiveXID = PrescanPreparedTransactions(&xids, &nxids);
5834			else
5835				oldestActiveXID = checkPoint.oldestActiveXid;
5836			Assert(TransactionIdIsValid(oldestActiveXID));
5837
5838			/* Tell procarray about the range of xids it has to deal with */
5839			ProcArrayInitRecovery(XidFromFullTransactionId(TransamVariables->nextXid));
5840
5841			/*
5842			 * Startup subtrans only.  CLOG, MultiXact and commit timestamp
5843			 * have already been started up and other SLRUs are not maintained
5844			 * during recovery and need not be started yet.
5845			 */
5846			StartupSUBTRANS(oldestActiveXID);
5847
5848			/*
5849			 * If we're beginning at a shutdown checkpoint, we know that
5850			 * nothing was running on the primary at this point. So fake-up an
5851			 * empty running-xacts record and use that here and now. Recover
5852			 * additional standby state for prepared transactions.
5853			 */
5854			if (wasShutdown)
5855			{
5856				RunningTransactionsData running;
5857				TransactionId latestCompletedXid;
5858
5859				/* Update pg_subtrans entries for any prepared transactions */
5860				StandbyRecoverPreparedTransactions();
5861
5862				/*
5863				 * Construct a RunningTransactions snapshot representing a
5864				 * shut down server, with only prepared transactions still
5865				 * alive. We're never overflowed at this point because all
5866				 * subxids are listed with their parent prepared transactions.
5867				 */
5868				running.xcnt = nxids;
5869				running.subxcnt = 0;
5870				running.subxid_status = SUBXIDS_IN_SUBTRANS;
5871				running.nextXid = XidFromFullTransactionId(checkPoint.nextXid);
5872				running.oldestRunningXid = oldestActiveXID;
5873				latestCompletedXid = XidFromFullTransactionId(checkPoint.nextXid);
5874				TransactionIdRetreat(latestCompletedXid);
5875				Assert(TransactionIdIsNormal(latestCompletedXid));
5876				running.latestCompletedXid = latestCompletedXid;
5877				running.xids = xids;
5878
5879				ProcArrayApplyRecoveryInfo(&running);
5880			}
5881		}
5882
5883		/*
5884		 * We're all set for replaying the WAL now. Do it.
5885		 */
5886		PerformWalRecovery();
5887		performedWalRecovery = true;
5888	}
5889	else
5890		performedWalRecovery = false;
5891
5892	/*
5893	 * Finish WAL recovery.
5894	 */
5895	endOfRecoveryInfo = FinishWalRecovery();
5896	EndOfLog = endOfRecoveryInfo->endOfLog;
5897	EndOfLogTLI = endOfRecoveryInfo->endOfLogTLI;
5898	abortedRecPtr = endOfRecoveryInfo->abortedRecPtr;
5899	missingContrecPtr = endOfRecoveryInfo->missingContrecPtr;
5900
5901	/*
5902	 * Reset ps status display, so as no information related to recovery shows
5903	 * up.
5904	 */
5905	set_ps_display("");
5906
5907	/*
5908	 * When recovering from a backup (we are in recovery, and archive recovery
5909	 * was requested), complain if we did not roll forward far enough to reach
5910	 * the point where the database is consistent.  For regular online
5911	 * backup-from-primary, that means reaching the end-of-backup WAL record
5912	 * (at which point we reset backupStartPoint to be Invalid), for
5913	 * backup-from-replica (which can't inject records into the WAL stream),
5914	 * that point is when we reach the minRecoveryPoint in pg_control (which
5915	 * we purposefully copy last when backing up from a replica).  For
5916	 * pg_rewind (which creates a backup_label with a method of "pg_rewind")
5917	 * or snapshot-style backups (which don't), backupEndRequired will be set
5918	 * to false.
5919	 *
5920	 * Note: it is indeed okay to look at the local variable
5921	 * LocalMinRecoveryPoint here, even though ControlFile->minRecoveryPoint
5922	 * might be further ahead --- ControlFile->minRecoveryPoint cannot have
5923	 * been advanced beyond the WAL we processed.
5924	 */
5925	if (InRecovery &&
5926		(EndOfLog < LocalMinRecoveryPoint ||
5927		 !XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(ControlFile->backupStartPoint)))
5928	{
5929		/*
5930		 * Ran off end of WAL before reaching end-of-backup WAL record, or
5931		 * minRecoveryPoint. That's a bad sign, indicating that you tried to
5932		 * recover from an online backup but never called pg_backup_stop(), or
5933		 * you didn't archive all the WAL needed.
5934		 */
5935		if (ArchiveRecoveryRequested || ControlFile->backupEndRequired)
5936		{
5937			if (!XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(ControlFile->backupStartPoint) || ControlFile->backupEndRequired)
5938				ereport(FATAL,
5939						(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
5940						 errmsg("WAL ends before end of online backup"),
5941						 errhint("All WAL generated while online backup was taken must be available at recovery.")));
5942			else
5943				ereport(FATAL,
5944						(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
5945						 errmsg("WAL ends before consistent recovery point")));
5946		}
5947	}
5948
5949	/*
5950	 * Reset unlogged relations to the contents of their INIT fork. This is
5951	 * done AFTER recovery is complete so as to include any unlogged relations
5952	 * created during recovery, but BEFORE recovery is marked as having
5953	 * completed successfully. Otherwise we'd not retry if any of the post
5954	 * end-of-recovery steps fail.
5955	 */
5956	if (InRecovery)
5957		ResetUnloggedRelations(UNLOGGED_RELATION_INIT);
5958
5959	/*
5960	 * Pre-scan prepared transactions to find out the range of XIDs present.
5961	 * This information is not quite needed yet, but it is positioned here so
5962	 * as potential problems are detected before any on-disk change is done.
5963	 */
5964	oldestActiveXID = PrescanPreparedTransactions(NULL, NULL);
5965
5966	/*
5967	 * Allow ordinary WAL segment creation before possibly switching to a new
5968	 * timeline, which creates a new segment, and after the last ReadRecord().
5969	 */
5970	SetInstallXLogFileSegmentActive();
5971
5972	/*
5973	 * Consider whether we need to assign a new timeline ID.
5974	 *
5975	 * If we did archive recovery, we always assign a new ID.  This handles a
5976	 * couple of issues.  If we stopped short of the end of WAL during
5977	 * recovery, then we are clearly generating a new timeline and must assign
5978	 * it a unique new ID.  Even if we ran to the end, modifying the current
5979	 * last segment is problematic because it may result in trying to
5980	 * overwrite an already-archived copy of that segment, and we encourage
5981	 * DBAs to make their archive_commands reject that.  We can dodge the
5982	 * problem by making the new active segment have a new timeline ID.
5983	 *
5984	 * In a normal crash recovery, we can just extend the timeline we were in.
5985	 */
5986	newTLI = endOfRecoveryInfo->lastRecTLI;
5987	if (ArchiveRecoveryRequested)
5988	{
5989		newTLI = findNewestTimeLine(recoveryTargetTLI) + 1;
5990		ereport(LOG,
5991				(errmsg("selected new timeline ID: %u", newTLI)));
5992
5993		/*
5994		 * Make a writable copy of the last WAL segment.  (Note that we also
5995		 * have a copy of the last block of the old WAL in
5996		 * endOfRecovery->lastPage; we will use that below.)
5997		 */
5998		XLogInitNewTimeline(EndOfLogTLI, EndOfLog, newTLI);
5999
6000		/*
6001		 * Remove the signal files out of the way, so that we don't
6002		 * accidentally re-enter archive recovery mode in a subsequent crash.
6003		 */
6004		if (endOfRecoveryInfo->standby_signal_file_found)
6005			durable_unlink(STANDBY_SIGNAL_FILE, FATAL);
6006
6007		if (endOfRecoveryInfo->recovery_signal_file_found)
6008			durable_unlink(RECOVERY_SIGNAL_FILE, FATAL);
6009
6010		/*
6011		 * Write the timeline history file, and have it archived. After this
6012		 * point (or rather, as soon as the file is archived), the timeline
6013		 * will appear as "taken" in the WAL archive and to any standby
6014		 * servers.  If we crash before actually switching to the new
6015		 * timeline, standby servers will nevertheless think that we switched
6016		 * to the new timeline, and will try to connect to the new timeline.
6017		 * To minimize the window for that, try to do as little as possible
6018		 * between here and writing the end-of-recovery record.
6019		 */
6020		writeTimeLineHistory(newTLI, recoveryTargetTLI,
6021							 EndOfLog, endOfRecoveryInfo->recoveryStopReason);
6022
6023		ereport(LOG,
6024				(errmsg("archive recovery complete")));
6025	}
6026
6027	/* Save the selected TimeLineID in shared memory, too */
6028	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6029	XLogCtl->InsertTimeLineID = newTLI;
6030	XLogCtl->PrevTimeLineID = endOfRecoveryInfo->lastRecTLI;
6031	SpinLockRelease(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6032
6033	/*
6034	 * Actually, if WAL ended in an incomplete record, skip the parts that
6035	 * made it through and start writing after the portion that persisted.
6036	 * (It's critical to first write an OVERWRITE_CONTRECORD message, which
6037	 * we'll do as soon as we're open for writing new WAL.)
6038	 */
6039	if (!XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(missingContrecPtr))
6040	{
6041		/*
6042		 * We should only have a missingContrecPtr if we're not switching to a
6043		 * new timeline. When a timeline switch occurs, WAL is copied from the
6044		 * old timeline to the new only up to the end of the last complete
6045		 * record, so there can't be an incomplete WAL record that we need to
6046		 * disregard.
6047		 */
6048		Assert(newTLI == endOfRecoveryInfo->lastRecTLI);
6049		Assert(!XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(abortedRecPtr));
6050		EndOfLog = missingContrecPtr;
6051	}
6052
6053	/*
6054	 * Prepare to write WAL starting at EndOfLog location, and init xlog
6055	 * buffer cache using the block containing the last record from the
6056	 * previous incarnation.
6057	 */
6058	Insert = &XLogCtl->Insert;
6059	Insert->PrevBytePos = XLogRecPtrToBytePos(endOfRecoveryInfo->lastRec);
6060	Insert->CurrBytePos = XLogRecPtrToBytePos(EndOfLog);
6061
6062	/*
6063	 * Tricky point here: lastPage contains the *last* block that the LastRec
6064	 * record spans, not the one it starts in.  The last block is indeed the
6065	 * one we want to use.
6066	 */
6067	if (EndOfLog % XLOG_BLCKSZ != 0)
6068	{
6069		char	   *page;
6070		int			len;
6071		int			firstIdx;
6072
6073		firstIdx = XLogRecPtrToBufIdx(EndOfLog);
6074		len = EndOfLog - endOfRecoveryInfo->lastPageBeginPtr;
6075		Assert(len < XLOG_BLCKSZ);
6076
6077		/* Copy the valid part of the last block, and zero the rest */
6078		page = &XLogCtl->pages[firstIdx * XLOG_BLCKSZ];
6079		memcpy(page, endOfRecoveryInfo->lastPage, len);
6080		memset(page + len, 0, XLOG_BLCKSZ - len);
6081
6082		pg_atomic_write_u64(&XLogCtl->xlblocks[firstIdx], endOfRecoveryInfo->lastPageBeginPtr + XLOG_BLCKSZ);
6083		XLogCtl->InitializedUpTo = endOfRecoveryInfo->lastPageBeginPtr + XLOG_BLCKSZ;
6084	}
6085	else
6086	{
6087		/*
6088		 * There is no partial block to copy. Just set InitializedUpTo, and
6089		 * let the first attempt to insert a log record to initialize the next
6090		 * buffer.
6091		 */
6092		XLogCtl->InitializedUpTo = EndOfLog;
6093	}
6094
6095	/*
6096	 * Update local and shared status.  This is OK to do without any locks
6097	 * because no other process can be reading or writing WAL yet.
6098	 */
6099	LogwrtResult.Write = LogwrtResult.Flush = EndOfLog;
6100	pg_atomic_write_u64(&XLogCtl->logInsertResult, EndOfLog);
6101	pg_atomic_write_u64(&XLogCtl->logWriteResult, EndOfLog);
6102	pg_atomic_write_u64(&XLogCtl->logFlushResult, EndOfLog);
6103	XLogCtl->LogwrtRqst.Write = EndOfLog;
6104	XLogCtl->LogwrtRqst.Flush = EndOfLog;
6105
6106	/*
6107	 * Preallocate additional log files, if wanted.
6108	 */
6109	PreallocXlogFiles(EndOfLog, newTLI);
6110
6111	/*
6112	 * Okay, we're officially UP.
6113	 */
6114	InRecovery = false;
6115
6116	/* start the archive_timeout timer and LSN running */
6117	XLogCtl->lastSegSwitchTime = (pg_time_t) time(NULL);
6118	XLogCtl->lastSegSwitchLSN = EndOfLog;
6119
6120	/* also initialize latestCompletedXid, to nextXid - 1 */
6121	LWLockAcquire(ProcArrayLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
6122	TransamVariables->latestCompletedXid = TransamVariables->nextXid;
6123	FullTransactionIdRetreat(&TransamVariables->latestCompletedXid);
6124	LWLockRelease(ProcArrayLock);
6125
6126	/*
6127	 * Start up subtrans, if not already done for hot standby.  (commit
6128	 * timestamps are started below, if necessary.)
6129	 */
6130	if (standbyState == STANDBY_DISABLED)
6131		StartupSUBTRANS(oldestActiveXID);
6132
6133	/*
6134	 * Perform end of recovery actions for any SLRUs that need it.
6135	 */
6136	TrimCLOG();
6137	TrimMultiXact();
6138
6139	/*
6140	 * Reload shared-memory state for prepared transactions.  This needs to
6141	 * happen before renaming the last partial segment of the old timeline as
6142	 * it may be possible that we have to recover some transactions from it.
6143	 */
6144	RecoverPreparedTransactions();
6145
6146	/* Shut down xlogreader */
6147	ShutdownWalRecovery();
6148
6149	/* Enable WAL writes for this backend only. */
6150	LocalSetXLogInsertAllowed();
6151
6152	/* If necessary, write overwrite-contrecord before doing anything else */
6153	if (!XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(abortedRecPtr))
6154	{
6155		Assert(!XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(missingContrecPtr));
6156		CreateOverwriteContrecordRecord(abortedRecPtr, missingContrecPtr, newTLI);
6157	}
6158
6159	/*
6160	 * Update full_page_writes in shared memory and write an XLOG_FPW_CHANGE
6161	 * record before resource manager writes cleanup WAL records or checkpoint
6162	 * record is written.
6163	 */
6164	Insert->fullPageWrites = lastFullPageWrites;
6165	UpdateFullPageWrites();
6166
6167	/*
6168	 * Emit checkpoint or end-of-recovery record in XLOG, if required.
6169	 */
6170	if (performedWalRecovery)
6171		promoted = PerformRecoveryXLogAction();
6172
6173	/*
6174	 * If any of the critical GUCs have changed, log them before we allow
6175	 * backends to write WAL.
6176	 */
6177	XLogReportParameters();
6178
6179	/* If this is archive recovery, perform post-recovery cleanup actions. */
6180	if (ArchiveRecoveryRequested)
6181		CleanupAfterArchiveRecovery(EndOfLogTLI, EndOfLog, newTLI);
6182
6183	/*
6184	 * Local WAL inserts enabled, so it's time to finish initialization of
6185	 * commit timestamp.
6186	 */
6187	CompleteCommitTsInitialization();
6188
6189	/*
6190	 * All done with end-of-recovery actions.
6191	 *
6192	 * Now allow backends to write WAL and update the control file status in
6193	 * consequence.  SharedRecoveryState, that controls if backends can write
6194	 * WAL, is updated while holding ControlFileLock to prevent other backends
6195	 * to look at an inconsistent state of the control file in shared memory.
6196	 * There is still a small window during which backends can write WAL and
6197	 * the control file is still referring to a system not in DB_IN_PRODUCTION
6198	 * state while looking at the on-disk control file.
6199	 *
6200	 * Also, we use info_lck to update SharedRecoveryState to ensure that
6201	 * there are no race conditions concerning visibility of other recent
6202	 * updates to shared memory.
6203	 */
6204	LWLockAcquire(ControlFileLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
6205	ControlFile->state = DB_IN_PRODUCTION;
6206
6207	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6208	XLogCtl->SharedRecoveryState = RECOVERY_STATE_DONE;
6209	SpinLockRelease(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6210
6211	UpdateControlFile();
6212	LWLockRelease(ControlFileLock);
6213
6214	/*
6215	 * Shutdown the recovery environment.  This must occur after
6216	 * RecoverPreparedTransactions() (see notes in lock_twophase_recover())
6217	 * and after switching SharedRecoveryState to RECOVERY_STATE_DONE so as
6218	 * any session building a snapshot will not rely on KnownAssignedXids as
6219	 * RecoveryInProgress() would return false at this stage.  This is
6220	 * particularly critical for prepared 2PC transactions, that would still
6221	 * need to be included in snapshots once recovery has ended.
6222	 */
6223	if (standbyState != STANDBY_DISABLED)
6224		ShutdownRecoveryTransactionEnvironment();
6225
6226	/*
6227	 * If there were cascading standby servers connected to us, nudge any wal
6228	 * sender processes to notice that we've been promoted.
6229	 */
6230	WalSndWakeup(true, true);
6231
6232	/*
6233	 * If this was a promotion, request an (online) checkpoint now. This isn't
6234	 * required for consistency, but the last restartpoint might be far back,
6235	 * and in case of a crash, recovering from it might take a longer than is
6236	 * appropriate now that we're not in standby mode anymore.
6237	 */
6238	if (promoted)
6239		RequestCheckpoint(CHECKPOINT_FORCE);
6240}
6241
6242/*
6243 * Callback from PerformWalRecovery(), called when we switch from crash
6244 * recovery to archive recovery mode.  Updates the control file accordingly.
6245 */
6246void
6247SwitchIntoArchiveRecovery(XLogRecPtr EndRecPtr, TimeLineID replayTLI)
6248{
6249	/* initialize minRecoveryPoint to this record */
6250	LWLockAcquire(ControlFileLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
6251	ControlFile->state = DB_IN_ARCHIVE_RECOVERY;
6252	if (ControlFile->minRecoveryPoint < EndRecPtr)
6253	{
6254		ControlFile->minRecoveryPoint = EndRecPtr;
6255		ControlFile->minRecoveryPointTLI = replayTLI;
6256	}
6257	/* update local copy */
6258	LocalMinRecoveryPoint = ControlFile->minRecoveryPoint;
6259	LocalMinRecoveryPointTLI = ControlFile->minRecoveryPointTLI;
6260
6261	/*
6262	 * The startup process can update its local copy of minRecoveryPoint from
6263	 * this point.
6264	 */
6265	updateMinRecoveryPoint = true;
6266
6267	UpdateControlFile();
6268
6269	/*
6270	 * We update SharedRecoveryState while holding the lock on ControlFileLock
6271	 * so both states are consistent in shared memory.
6272	 */
6273	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6274	XLogCtl->SharedRecoveryState = RECOVERY_STATE_ARCHIVE;
6275	SpinLockRelease(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6276
6277	LWLockRelease(ControlFileLock);
6278}
6279
6280/*
6281 * Callback from PerformWalRecovery(), called when we reach the end of backup.
6282 * Updates the control file accordingly.
6283 */
6284void
6285ReachedEndOfBackup(XLogRecPtr EndRecPtr, TimeLineID tli)
6286{
6287	/*
6288	 * We have reached the end of base backup, as indicated by pg_control. The
6289	 * data on disk is now consistent (unless minRecoveryPoint is further
6290	 * ahead, which can happen if we crashed during previous recovery).  Reset
6291	 * backupStartPoint and backupEndPoint, and update minRecoveryPoint to
6292	 * make sure we don't allow starting up at an earlier point even if
6293	 * recovery is stopped and restarted soon after this.
6294	 */
6295	LWLockAcquire(ControlFileLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
6296
6297	if (ControlFile->minRecoveryPoint < EndRecPtr)
6298	{
6299		ControlFile->minRecoveryPoint = EndRecPtr;
6300		ControlFile->minRecoveryPointTLI = tli;
6301	}
6302
6303	ControlFile->backupStartPoint = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
6304	ControlFile->backupEndPoint = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
6305	ControlFile->backupEndRequired = false;
6306	UpdateControlFile();
6307
6308	LWLockRelease(ControlFileLock);
6309}
6310
6311/*
6312 * Perform whatever XLOG actions are necessary at end of REDO.
6313 *
6314 * The goal here is to make sure that we'll be able to recover properly if
6315 * we crash again. If we choose to write a checkpoint, we'll write a shutdown
6316 * checkpoint rather than an on-line one. This is not particularly critical,
6317 * but since we may be assigning a new TLI, using a shutdown checkpoint allows
6318 * us to have the rule that TLI only changes in shutdown checkpoints, which
6319 * allows some extra error checking in xlog_redo.
6320 */
6321static bool
6322PerformRecoveryXLogAction(void)
6323{
6324	bool		promoted = false;
6325
6326	/*
6327	 * Perform a checkpoint to update all our recovery activity to disk.
6328	 *
6329	 * Note that we write a shutdown checkpoint rather than an on-line one.
6330	 * This is not particularly critical, but since we may be assigning a new
6331	 * TLI, using a shutdown checkpoint allows us to have the rule that TLI
6332	 * only changes in shutdown checkpoints, which allows some extra error
6333	 * checking in xlog_redo.
6334	 *
6335	 * In promotion, only create a lightweight end-of-recovery record instead
6336	 * of a full checkpoint. A checkpoint is requested later, after we're
6337	 * fully out of recovery mode and already accepting queries.
6338	 */
6339	if (ArchiveRecoveryRequested && IsUnderPostmaster &&
6340		PromoteIsTriggered())
6341	{
6342		promoted = true;
6343
6344		/*
6345		 * Insert a special WAL record to mark the end of recovery, since we
6346		 * aren't doing a checkpoint. That means that the checkpointer process
6347		 * may likely be in the middle of a time-smoothed restartpoint and
6348		 * could continue to be for minutes after this.  That sounds strange,
6349		 * but the effect is roughly the same and it would be stranger to try
6350		 * to come out of the restartpoint and then checkpoint. We request a
6351		 * checkpoint later anyway, just for safety.
6352		 */
6353		CreateEndOfRecoveryRecord();
6354	}
6355	else
6356	{
6357		RequestCheckpoint(CHECKPOINT_END_OF_RECOVERY |
6358						  CHECKPOINT_IMMEDIATE |
6359						  CHECKPOINT_WAIT);
6360	}
6361
6362	return promoted;
6363}
6364
6365/*
6366 * Is the system still in recovery?
6367 *
6368 * Unlike testing InRecovery, this works in any process that's connected to
6369 * shared memory.
6370 */
6371bool
6372RecoveryInProgress(void)
6373{
6374	/*
6375	 * We check shared state each time only until we leave recovery mode. We
6376	 * can't re-enter recovery, so there's no need to keep checking after the
6377	 * shared variable has once been seen false.
6378	 */
6379	if (!LocalRecoveryInProgress)
6380		return false;
6381	else
6382	{
6383		/*
6384		 * use volatile pointer to make sure we make a fresh read of the
6385		 * shared variable.
6386		 */
6387		volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
6388
6389		LocalRecoveryInProgress = (xlogctl->SharedRecoveryState != RECOVERY_STATE_DONE);
6390
6391		/*
6392		 * Note: We don't need a memory barrier when we're still in recovery.
6393		 * We might exit recovery immediately after return, so the caller
6394		 * can't rely on 'true' meaning that we're still in recovery anyway.
6395		 */
6396
6397		return LocalRecoveryInProgress;
6398	}
6399}
6400
6401/*
6402 * Returns current recovery state from shared memory.
6403 *
6404 * This returned state is kept consistent with the contents of the control
6405 * file.  See details about the possible values of RecoveryState in xlog.h.
6406 */
6407RecoveryState
6408GetRecoveryState(void)
6409{
6410	RecoveryState retval;
6411
6412	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6413	retval = XLogCtl->SharedRecoveryState;
6414	SpinLockRelease(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6415
6416	return retval;
6417}
6418
6419/*
6420 * Is this process allowed to insert new WAL records?
6421 *
6422 * Ordinarily this is essentially equivalent to !RecoveryInProgress().
6423 * But we also have provisions for forcing the result "true" or "false"
6424 * within specific processes regardless of the global state.
6425 */
6426bool
6427XLogInsertAllowed(void)
6428{
6429	/*
6430	 * If value is "unconditionally true" or "unconditionally false", just
6431	 * return it.  This provides the normal fast path once recovery is known
6432	 * done.
6433	 */
6434	if (LocalXLogInsertAllowed >= 0)
6435		return (bool) LocalXLogInsertAllowed;
6436
6437	/*
6438	 * Else, must check to see if we're still in recovery.
6439	 */
6440	if (RecoveryInProgress())
6441		return false;
6442
6443	/*
6444	 * On exit from recovery, reset to "unconditionally true", since there is
6445	 * no need to keep checking.
6446	 */
6447	LocalXLogInsertAllowed = 1;
6448	return true;
6449}
6450
6451/*
6452 * Make XLogInsertAllowed() return true in the current process only.
6453 *
6454 * Note: it is allowed to switch LocalXLogInsertAllowed back to -1 later,
6455 * and even call LocalSetXLogInsertAllowed() again after that.
6456 *
6457 * Returns the previous value of LocalXLogInsertAllowed.
6458 */
6459static int
6460LocalSetXLogInsertAllowed(void)
6461{
6462	int			oldXLogAllowed = LocalXLogInsertAllowed;
6463
6464	LocalXLogInsertAllowed = 1;
6465
6466	return oldXLogAllowed;
6467}
6468
6469/*
6470 * Return the current Redo pointer from shared memory.
6471 *
6472 * As a side-effect, the local RedoRecPtr copy is updated.
6473 */
6474XLogRecPtr
6475GetRedoRecPtr(void)
6476{
6477	XLogRecPtr	ptr;
6478
6479	/*
6480	 * The possibly not up-to-date copy in XlogCtl is enough. Even if we
6481	 * grabbed a WAL insertion lock to read the authoritative value in
6482	 * Insert->RedoRecPtr, someone might update it just after we've released
6483	 * the lock.
6484	 */
6485	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6486	ptr = XLogCtl->RedoRecPtr;
6487	SpinLockRelease(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6488
6489	if (RedoRecPtr < ptr)
6490		RedoRecPtr = ptr;
6491
6492	return RedoRecPtr;
6493}
6494
6495/*
6496 * Return information needed to decide whether a modified block needs a
6497 * full-page image to be included in the WAL record.
6498 *
6499 * The returned values are cached copies from backend-private memory, and
6500 * possibly out-of-date or, indeed, uninitialized, in which case they will
6501 * be InvalidXLogRecPtr and false, respectively.  XLogInsertRecord will
6502 * re-check them against up-to-date values, while holding the WAL insert lock.
6503 */
6504void
6505GetFullPageWriteInfo(XLogRecPtr *RedoRecPtr_p, bool *doPageWrites_p)
6506{
6507	*RedoRecPtr_p = RedoRecPtr;
6508	*doPageWrites_p = doPageWrites;
6509}
6510
6511/*
6512 * GetInsertRecPtr -- Returns the current insert position.
6513 *
6514 * NOTE: The value *actually* returned is the position of the last full
6515 * xlog page. It lags behind the real insert position by at most 1 page.
6516 * For that, we don't need to scan through WAL insertion locks, and an
6517 * approximation is enough for the current usage of this function.
6518 */
6519XLogRecPtr
6520GetInsertRecPtr(void)
6521{
6522	XLogRecPtr	recptr;
6523
6524	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6525	recptr = XLogCtl->LogwrtRqst.Write;
6526	SpinLockRelease(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6527
6528	return recptr;
6529}
6530
6531/*
6532 * GetFlushRecPtr -- Returns the current flush position, ie, the last WAL
6533 * position known to be fsync'd to disk. This should only be used on a
6534 * system that is known not to be in recovery.
6535 */
6536XLogRecPtr
6537GetFlushRecPtr(TimeLineID *insertTLI)
6538{
6539	Assert(XLogCtl->SharedRecoveryState == RECOVERY_STATE_DONE);
6540
6541	RefreshXLogWriteResult(LogwrtResult);
6542
6543	/*
6544	 * If we're writing and flushing WAL, the time line can't be changing, so
6545	 * no lock is required.
6546	 */
6547	if (insertTLI)
6548		*insertTLI = XLogCtl->InsertTimeLineID;
6549
6550	return LogwrtResult.Flush;
6551}
6552
6553/*
6554 * GetWALInsertionTimeLine -- Returns the current timeline of a system that
6555 * is not in recovery.
6556 */
6557TimeLineID
6558GetWALInsertionTimeLine(void)
6559{
6560	Assert(XLogCtl->SharedRecoveryState == RECOVERY_STATE_DONE);
6561
6562	/* Since the value can't be changing, no lock is required. */
6563	return XLogCtl->InsertTimeLineID;
6564}
6565
6566/*
6567 * GetWALInsertionTimeLineIfSet -- If the system is not in recovery, returns
6568 * the WAL insertion timeline; else, returns 0. Wherever possible, use
6569 * GetWALInsertionTimeLine() instead, since it's cheaper. Note that this
6570 * function decides recovery has ended as soon as the insert TLI is set, which
6571 * happens before we set XLogCtl->SharedRecoveryState to RECOVERY_STATE_DONE.
6572 */
6573TimeLineID
6574GetWALInsertionTimeLineIfSet(void)
6575{
6576	TimeLineID	insertTLI;
6577
6578	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6579	insertTLI = XLogCtl->InsertTimeLineID;
6580	SpinLockRelease(&XLogCtl->info_lck);
6581
6582	return insertTLI;
6583}
6584
6585/*
6586 * GetLastImportantRecPtr -- Returns the LSN of the last important record
6587 * inserted. All records not explicitly marked as unimportant are considered
6588 * important.
6589 *
6590 * The LSN is determined by computing the maximum of
6591 * WALInsertLocks[i].lastImportantAt.
6592 */
6593XLogRecPtr
6594GetLastImportantRecPtr(void)
6595{
6596	XLogRecPtr	res = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
6597	int			i;
6598
6599	for (i = 0; i < NUM_XLOGINSERT_LOCKS; i++)
6600	{
6601		XLogRecPtr	last_important;
6602
6603		/*
6604		 * Need to take a lock to prevent torn reads of the LSN, which are
6605		 * possible on some of the supported platforms. WAL insert locks only
6606		 * support exclusive mode, so we have to use that.
6607		 */
6608		LWLockAcquire(&WALInsertLocks[i].l.lock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
6609		last_important = WALInsertLocks[i].l.lastImportantAt;
6610		LWLockRelease(&WALInsertLocks[i].l.lock);
6611
6612		if (res < last_important)
6613			res = last_important;
6614	}
6615
6616	return res;
6617}
6618
6619/*
6620 * Get the time and LSN of the last xlog segment switch
6621 */
6622pg_time_t
6623GetLastSegSwitchData(XLogRecPtr *lastSwitchLSN)
6624{
6625	pg_time_t	result;
6626
6627	/* Need WALWriteLock, but shared lock is sufficient */
6628	LWLockAcquire(WALWriteLock, LW_SHARED);
6629	result = XLogCtl->lastSegSwitchTime;
6630	*lastSwitchLSN = XLogCtl->lastSegSwitchLSN;
6631	LWLockRelease(WALWriteLock);
6632
6633	return result;
6634}
6635
6636/*
6637 * This must be called ONCE during postmaster or standalone-backend shutdown
6638 */
6639void
6640ShutdownXLOG(int code, Datum arg)
6641{
6642	/*
6643	 * We should have an aux process resource owner to use, and we should not
6644	 * be in a transaction that's installed some other resowner.
6645	 */
6646	Assert(AuxProcessResourceOwner != NULL);
6647	Assert(CurrentResourceOwner == NULL ||
6648		   CurrentResourceOwner == AuxProcessResourceOwner);
6649	CurrentResourceOwner = AuxProcessResourceOwner;
6650
6651	/* Don't be chatty in standalone mode */
6652	ereport(IsPostmasterEnvironment ? LOG : NOTICE,
6653			(errmsg("shutting down")));
6654
6655	/*
6656	 * Signal walsenders to move to stopping state.
6657	 */
6658	WalSndInitStopping();
6659
6660	/*
6661	 * Wait for WAL senders to be in stopping state.  This prevents commands
6662	 * from writing new WAL.
6663	 */
6664	WalSndWaitStopping();
6665
6666	if (RecoveryInProgress())
6667		CreateRestartPoint(CHECKPOINT_IS_SHUTDOWN | CHECKPOINT_IMMEDIATE);
6668	else
6669	{
6670		/*
6671		 * If archiving is enabled, rotate the last XLOG file so that all the
6672		 * remaining records are archived (postmaster wakes up the archiver
6673		 * process one more time at the end of shutdown). The checkpoint
6674		 * record will go to the next XLOG file and won't be archived (yet).
6675		 */
6676		if (XLogArchivingActive())
6677			RequestXLogSwitch(false);
6678
6679		CreateCheckPoint(CHECKPOINT_IS_SHUTDOWN | CHECKPOINT_IMMEDIATE);
6680	}
6681}
6682
6683/*
6684 * Log start of a checkpoint.
6685 */
6686static void
6687LogCheckpointStart(int flags, bool restartpoint)
6688{
6689	if (restartpoint)
6690		ereport(LOG,
6691		/* translator: the placeholders show checkpoint options */
6692				(errmsg("restartpoint starting:%s%s%s%s%s%s%s%s",
6693						(flags & CHECKPOINT_IS_SHUTDOWN) ? " shutdown" : "",
6694						(flags & CHECKPOINT_END_OF_RECOVERY) ? " end-of-recovery" : "",
6695						(flags & CHECKPOINT_IMMEDIATE) ? " immediate" : "",
6696						(flags & CHECKPOINT_FORCE) ? " force" : "",
6697						(flags & CHECKPOINT_WAIT) ? " wait" : "",
6698						(flags & CHECKPOINT_CAUSE_XLOG) ? " wal" : "",
